title: Shared launcher-icon generator for the Flutter apps
objective: Replace the stock Flutter 'beach' launcher icon on every Flutter/Dart app with an icon from one consistent family, and make the family reproducible so future apps inherit it instead of drifting. The family is a charcoal #1B1D21 field carrying a single glyph in that app's own theme accent, drawn at one stroke weight and optically centred so a row of the icons lines up. Success means the phone launcher shows five distinct, on-style icons and a new app can be given a matching icon by adding one glyph plus one registry entry.
acceptance_criteria: python_pkg/app_icons exposes a CLI with list, preview and generate subcommands, runnable as PYTHONPATH=~/testsAndMisc python3 -m python_pkg.app_icons.
Glyphs are optically centred automatically: the renderer measures rendered ink and translates (never scales) the fragment, so stroke weight stays identical across the family.
generate warns when a glyph's ink exceeds the 560px adaptive-icon safe box, so no icon ships at risk of being clipped by a launcher mask.
Each app gets three layers - base icon, adaptive foreground, and Android 13 monochrome - as both SVG source and 1024px PNG.
The package passes ruff, mypy, pylint and bandit, and holds 100% line and branch coverage with all external tools mocked.
All five apps show their new icon in the phone app drawer, installed with adb install -r and with no app data wiped.
out_of_scope: The per-app repositories (dufs-cloud, screen-locker, wake-alarm, diet-guard, todo) - their generated assets are committed separately in their own repos.
iOS, web, Windows and macOS icon targets - none of these apps configure those platforms.
Changing any app's android:label or in-app theme colours; this is icons only.
verifier: pre-commit run --files <staged>; python3 -m pytest python_pkg/app_icons --cov=python_pkg.app_icons --cov-report=term-missing (100% required); plus on-device verification: adb install -r each app then screenshot the launcher.
